paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: The saiga, a species of antelope native to Central Asia, once roamed the vast grasslands of this region in enormous herds, many millions strong. Regrettably, such spectacular sights are a thing of the past. Today, the saiga is largely confined to a single country: Kazakhstan. This country is estimated to be home to well over 90% of the global saiga population, with Russia, Mongolia and Uzbekistan accounting for the rest.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: The saiga is perfectly adapted to the tough conditions of the remote wilderness of the steppes of Central Asia. One such adaptation is its bizarre bulbous nose, which enables the animal to survive the extreme seasonal temperature swings of the region. The swollen nostrils of the nose serve several purposes: they filter out dust and cool the blood during hot, dry summers, and they warm the cold air before it enters the saiga's lungs in winter. Other seasonal adaptations include a heavy winter coat that the saiga sheds when the weather warms up.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Despite these superb adaptations to harsh conditions, the saiga has no defence against the threats posed by humans. It was almost driven to extinction by hunters in the 19th century. Legal protection ensured its survival for a while, and numbers steadily recovered throughout most of the 20th century. But the respite was only temporary. In the ten years following the break-up of the former Soviet Union in 1991, over 95% of the global population was lost - one of the fastest examples of species loss ever recorded for a mammal.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: The dramatic decline during this decade was due to illegal poaching on an industrial scale. Male saiga are a particular target, because their horns are highly prized by traditional medicine practitioners. Poaching reached epidemic levels after misguided conservationists tried to relieve the pressure on threatened African rhinos by actively encouraging the use of saiga horns in traditional medicine as an alternative to those of rhinos. Male saiga were almost wiped out, leading to a population crash from which the species has been struggling to recover ever since.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Another threat to the survival of the saiga is loss of habitat, as a result of agricultural expansion and human settlement. Physical barriers such as railways, pipelines and fences can block the seasonal migration routes of this transboundary species. In the worst cases, herds may starve to death after being trapped.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Then there is the risk of disease. In 2015, an outbreak of haemorrhagic septicaemia, caused by the normally harmless bacterium Pasteurella multocida, killed over 75% of the global adult saiga population in just three weeks. In 2017, 60% of the Mongolian saiga population - a subspecies found nowhere else in the world - was killed by a virus that spilled over from livestock. These so-called mass mortality events represent an unpredictable and serious threat to the species.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Climate change poses a further threat. Although well adapted to cold winters and hot summers, saiga struggle to cope with temperature extremes and unpredictable fluctuations in climate. Experts believe that unusually warm weather may have triggered the 2015 mass mortality event. The steppe region has also become increasingly arid in recent years, and many of the smaller streams that the species normally depended on have dried up and vanished.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Recent efforts to save the saiga have been spearheaded by the Altyn Dala Conservation Initiative, a project led by the Association for the Conservation of Biodiversity of Kazakhstan, working in partnership with the Kazakh government's Committee for Forestry and Wildlife, Frankfurt Zoological Society and Fauna and Flora, an international conservation charity. Its purpose is to protect and restore Kazakhstan's steppe, semi-desert and desert ecosystems and the many species they support, including the critically endangered saiga. In 2022 the United Nations recognised the initiative as a World Restoration Flagship project, an accolade reserved for the ten best examples of large-scale ecosystem restoration around the globe.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: So, how many saiga are there now? By 2000, the global saiga population had hit an all-time low of just 21,000 individuals. There was some recovery in the first decade of the new millennium but this was then crushed by devastating mass mortality events that saw the loss of hundreds of thousands of the species. But thanks to the intervention of the Altyn Dala Conservation Initiative, the most recent episodes in the ongoing story of the saiga have been relatively uplifting. Three years ago, the Ustyurt Plateau population in Kazakhstan experienced its largest mass birth of saiga calves in many years. An aerial census two years ago recorded an estimated 842,000 saiga across Kazakhstan as a whole, and according to an aerial survey earlier this year, the saiga population in Kazakhstan now exceeds 1.9 million. The world's strangest-looking antelope remains critically endangered, but the direction of travel is positive.

groups.title: The saiga
groups.desc:
Questions 1-7
Complete the notes below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
Write your answers in boxes 1-7 on your answer sheet.
groups.collect: Adaptations • has a large bulbous nose with swollen nostrils that - keep $40273$ out - lower the temperature of its $40274$ in summer - warm the air entering its lungs in winter • grows a thick $40275$ in winter, which it loses in spring Reasons for population decline • poaching, especially for the $40276$ of male saiga • expansion of farms and settlements, causing - reduction in the size of the saiga's $40277$ - loss of access to the $40278$ which they use for migration • various forms of disease, leading to mass mortality events • climate change, causing the disappearance of $40279$ which the saiga relied on

questions.analysis: 题干中的“keep ___ out”对应原文中的“filter out dust”，描述高鼻羚羊膨大的鼻孔在夏季能过滤灰尘。因此，此处应填入“dust”，表示其鼻子可阻挡灰尘进入体内。

central_sentences.analysis: 原文明确指出鼻子可filter out dust
sentences: The swollen nostrils of the nose serve several purposes: they filter out dust and cool the blood during hot, dry summers, and they warm the cold air before it enters the saiga's lungs in winter.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: keep dust out -> filter out dust

central_sentences.sentence: The swollen nostrils of the nose serve several purposes: they filter out dust and cool the blood during hot, dry summers, and they warm the cold air before it enters the saiga's lungs in winter.

questions.analysis: 题干中“lower the temperature of its ___ in summer”与原文“cool the blood during hot, dry summers”形成同义改写关系。根据上下文和关键词“blood”的直接出现，答案为“blood”。

central_sentences.analysis: 题干与原文表达一致，blood为唯一答案
sentences: The swollen nostrils of the nose serve several purposes: they filter out dust and cool the blood during hot, dry summers, and they warm the cold air before it enters the saiga's lungs in winter.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: lower the temperature of its blood -> cool the blood

central_sentences.sentence: The swollen nostrils of the nose serve several purposes: they filter out dust and cool the blood during hot, dry summers, and they warm the cold air before it enters the saiga's lungs in winter.

questions.analysis: 题干提到“grows a thick ___ in winter”，而原文指出“a heavy winter coat that the saiga sheds when the weather warms up”。其中“coat”是唯一符合“One Word Only”要求且语义匹配的答案。

central_sentences.analysis: coat对应原文winter coat，符合one word要求
sentences: Other seasonal adaptations include a heavy winter coat that the saiga sheds when the weather warms up.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: thick coat -> heavy winter coat

central_sentences.sentence: Other seasonal adaptations include a heavy winter coat that the saiga sheds when the weather warms up.

questions.analysis: 题干中“poaching, especially for the ___ of male saiga”对应原文“Male saiga are a particular target, because their horns are highly prized...”。由此可知，偷猎主要针对雄性高鼻羚羊的角，故答案为“horns”。

central_sentences.analysis: 雄性高鼻羚羊因horns被猎杀
sentences: Male saiga are a particular target, because their horns are highly prized by traditional medicine practitioners.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: poaching for horns -> horns are highly prized

central_sentences.sentence: Male saiga are a particular target, because their horns are highly prized by traditional medicine practitioners.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“reduction in the size of the saiga's ___”对应原文“loss of habitat, as a result of agricultural expansion and human settlement”。因此，“habitat”准确反映了因农业扩张导致的栖息地减少问题。

central_sentences.analysis: habitat直接对应原文loss of habitat
sentences: Another threat to the survival of the saiga is loss of habitat, as a result of agricultural expansion and human settlement.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: reduction in habitat size -> loss of habitat

central_sentences.sentence: Another threat to the survival of the saiga is loss of habitat, as a result of agricultural expansion and human settlement.

questions.analysis: 题干中“loss of access to the ___ which they use for migration”对应原文“block the seasonal migration routes”。这里的“routes”指高鼻羚羊迁徙所依赖的路径，被铁路、管道等障碍物阻断，因此答案为“routes”。

central_sentences.analysis: routes指代seasonal migration routes
sentences: Physical barriers such as railways, pipelines and fences can block the seasonal migration routes of this transboundary species.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: access to migration routes -> block the seasonal migration routes

central_sentences.sentence: Physical barriers such as railways, pipelines and fences can block the seasonal migration routes of this transboundary species.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“disappearance of ___ which the saiga relied on”对应原文“many of the smaller streams that the species normally depended on have dried up and vanished”。因此，答案“streams”准确描述了因气候干旱而消失的水源。

central_sentences.analysis: streams为saiga依赖的水源，现已消失
sentences: The steppe region has also become increasingly arid in recent years, and many of the smaller streams that the species normally depended on have dried up and vanished.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: disappearance of streams -> smaller streams ... have dried up

central_sentences.sentence: The steppe region has also become increasingly arid in recent years, and many of the smaller streams that the species normally depended on have dried up and vanished.

groups.desc:
Questions 8-13
Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 1?
In boxes 8-13 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this

questions.title: Today, numbers of saiga are distributed evenly across four nations in Central Asia: Kazakhstan, Russia, Mongolia and Uzbekistan.
questions.analysis: 题干称高鼻羚羊如今均匀分布在四个国家，但原文明确指出“Today, the saiga is largely confined to a single country: Kazakhstan”，其余三国仅占极小比例，说明分布并不均匀，因此该陈述错误，答案为FALSE。

central_sentences.analysis: 原文说90%以上在哈萨克斯坦，分布不均，故FALSE。
sentences: This country is estimated to be home to well over 90% of the global saiga population, with Russia, Mongolia and Uzbekistan accounting for the rest.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: distributed evenly -> home to well over 90% with rest in others

central_sentences.sentence: This country is estimated to be home to well over 90% of the global saiga population, with Russia, Mongolia and Uzbekistan accounting for the rest.

questions.title: For most of the 20th century, the population of saiga were falling.
questions.analysis: 题干说“20世纪大部分时间种群数量持续下降”，但原文提到“numbers steadily recovered throughout most of the 20th century”，即种群数量在20世纪大部分时间稳步恢复，与题干矛盾，故答案为FALSE。

central_sentences.analysis: 原文说20世纪大部分时间数量稳定恢复，故FALSE。
sentences: Legal protection ensured its survival for a while, and numbers steadily recovered throughout most of the 20th century.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: falling -> steadily recovered

central_sentences.sentence: Legal protection ensured its survival for a while, and numbers steadily recovered throughout most of the 20th century.

questions.title: Efforts to protect rhinos in Africa had a significant effect on saiga populations.
questions.analysis: 题干指出保护非洲犀牛的努力对高鼻羚羊种群产生重大影响，原文提到环保人士鼓励使用高鼻羚羊角替代犀牛角，结果导致雄性高鼻羚羊遭大规模偷猎，种群崩溃。因此该说法正确，答案为TRUE。

central_sentences.reason: 直接导致雄性赛加羚羊几乎灭绝
central_sentences.analysis: 原文说保护非洲犀牛导致赛加羚羊角被鼓励使用，雄性几乎灭绝，故TRUE。
sentences: Poaching reached epidemic levels after misguided conservationists tried to relieve the pressure on threatened African rhinos by actively encouraging the use of saiga horns in traditional medicine as an alternative to those of rhinos.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: efforts to protect rhinos -> actively encouraging the use of saiga horns

central_sentences.sentence: Poaching reached epidemic levels after misguided conservationists tried to relieve the pressure on threatened African rhinos by actively encouraging the use of saiga horns in traditional medicine as an alternative to those of rhinos.

questions.title: Unpredictable fluctuations in climate are threatening the wildlife of Central Asia more than in other parts of the world.
questions.analysis: 题干声称中亚野生动物受气候变化威胁比世界其他地区更严重，但原文仅说明气候变化对该物种构成威胁，并未与其他地区进行比较，因此文中无此信息，答案为NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.analysis: 原文只提到气候变化威胁，未与其他地区比较，故NOT GIVEN。
central_sentences.keywords: unpredictable fluctuations, threatening more than other
sentences: Although well adapted to cold winters and hot summers, saiga struggle to cope with temperature extremes and unpredictable fluctuations in climate.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: no comparison in passage

central_sentences.sentence: Although well adapted to cold winters and hot summers, saiga struggle to cope with temperature extremes and unpredictable fluctuations in climate.

questions.title: The Altyn Dala Conservation Initiative was formed for the benefit of a number of different animals.
questions.analysis: 题干称Altyn Dala保护倡议旨在造福多种动物，原文指出该项目目的是“protect and restore... ecosystems and the many species they support, including the critically endangered saiga”，表明其受益对象包括多个物种，故答案为TRUE。

central_sentences.analysis: 原文说保护多种物种，故TRUE。
central_sentences.keywords: Altyn Dala Conservation Initiative, benefit a number of different animals
sentences: Its purpose is to protect and restore Kazakhstan's steppe, semi-desert and desert ecosystems and the many species they support, including the critically endangered saiga.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: benefit of a number of different animals -> protect and restore ... the many species they support

central_sentences.sentence: Its purpose is to protect and restore Kazakhstan's steppe, semi-desert and desert ecosystems and the many species they support, including the critically endangered saiga.

questions.title: The Altyn Dala Conservation Initiative's recognition as a World Restoration Flagship project attracted additional international funding for the scheme.
questions.analysis: 题干认为该项目获得联合国认可后吸引了额外国际资金，但原文只提到它被列为“World Restoration Flagship project”，并未提及是否因此获得新资金支持，故答案为NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.analysis: 原文只提到被认可，未提资金，故NOT GIVEN。
central_sentences.keywords: World Restoration Flagship, additional international funding
sentences: In 2022 the United Nations recognised the initiative as a World Restoration Flagship project, an accolade reserved for the ten best examples of large-scale ecosystem restoration around the globe.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: attracted additional international funding -> no mention

central_sentences.sentence: In 2022 the United Nations recognised the initiative as a World Restoration Flagship project, an accolade reserved for the ten best examples of large-scale ecosystem restoration around the globe.

content.title: The problems of getting around the city of Dar es Salaam

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Dar es Salaam in Tanzania is one of the fastest growing cities in Africa. Its population has increased eightfold since 1980 and swells by half a million people every year. United Nations projections anticipate it will become a megacity within seven years as its population passes 10 million, reaching 13.4 million by 2035. Daniel Hoornweg for the Global Cities Institute forecasts the city could be home to an incredible 73.7 million people by 2100.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Today, four out of five of its people live in single-storey informal settlements on the spreading edges of the city, where the journey to and from the centre regularly takes over two hours. It can be longer if rain turns the dirt roads to mud.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Even in the middle of the day, traffic frequently slows to a stop without warning. It is not unusual for cars and minibuses to queue for 20 minutes at a key intersection. A single suburban rail line serves residents in a few areas to the south but is tiny in the context of the wider city. Outside the centre many rely on boda boda (motorbike taxis) to navigate the narrow side streets and potholed mud roads that make up much of the metropolis. Their safety record is scandalous.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Dar es Salaam's reliance on four arterial roads into the city is a legacy of the colonial government that planned the city at the start of the 20th century to cater for a population of 35,000. Most of the current growth is made up of young people arriving from the countryside to find work, and as the population has exploded, Dar es Salaam has grown around those four highways. Nearly all the expansion is happening on the periphery, and nearly all takes place informally without any agreed strategy.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: But Dar es Salaam is pinning its hopes on a solution that could offer a different model for Africa's megacities, giving them an alternative to a future controlled by the private car. Unlike many cities on the continent, Dar es Salaam is not trying to build a metro. It has chosen a less exciting but cheaper and more achievable method: the bus.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: The DART bus rapid transit (BRT) system runs on bus lanes separated from other traffic, mostly in the middle of the road to reduce stoppages. Ticket purchase and control takes place at stations prior to boarding and the buses are step-free, which means the entire route is accessible to people using wheelchairs or who are travelling with baby buggies.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: The new buses are much, much better, says Paulas George, a young IT worker. He takes the bus every day and it has cut his journey time by two-thirds. He says it is not perfect, though, complaining that drivers often refuse to turn on the air conditioning to save fuel.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: That is not the only problem. A shortage of buses after a serious flood at the main depot during the rainy season means the system is carrying 200,000 people a day - half the expected capacity. Smartcards cannot be used as the mechanical readers are not working either, forcing passengers to buy individual paper tickets for every journey. Each is printed with a scannable QR code, but there are no scanners. Staff stand by the gates and tear tickets as people enter. As a result, queues are considerable at peak times.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: Morogoro Road to the north-west of the city was phase I of the BRT project. Phases II and III will install bus lanes along Nyerere Road to the south-west and Kilwa Road to the south. Construction on both routes is due to start imminently. Phase IV, towards Bagamoyo in the north, is in the preliminary design stage. Much of the city will have access to a world-class transport system within the space of a few years, says Chris Kost, the Africa director of ITDP. All phases are being planned to high standards and, once complete, a third of city residents will be within a short walk of the BRT network.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: The ITDP regrets Africa's obsession with metros. With a metro, an international firm will often just parachute in its own system, says Kost. Bus rapid transit allows existing stakeholders to get involved. That is what we did in Dar es Salaam and what we are planning in Nairobi, where the bus bodies will be built in the city and local operators will look after tickets, fare collection and IT. Bus rapid transit has been transformational for Dar es Salaam. For millions of people in African cities, this is their best hope of ever being connected.

groups.desc:
Questions 14-18
Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 2?
In boxes 14-18 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this

questions.title: The population of Dar es Salaam is rising more rapidly than was previously predicted.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“more rapidly than was previously predicted”暗示存在新旧预测的对比，但原文仅提到联合国对未来人口的预测（如2035年达1340万），并未提及此前是否有不同或更保守的预测被超越。因此，文中缺乏判断该陈述真假所需的信息，答案为NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.analysis: 文中仅给出联合国预测，未提此前预测是否被超越
sentences: United Nations projections anticipate it will become a megacity within seven years as its population passes 10 million, reaching 13.4 million by 2035.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: previously predicted -> UN projections

central_sentences.sentence: United Nations projections anticipate it will become a megacity within seven years as its population passes 10 million, reaching 13.4 million by 2035.

questions.title: Most of the residents of Dar es Salaam live in high-rise blocks on the edge of the city.
questions.analysis: 题干称居民住在城市边缘的“high-rise blocks”（高层公寓），但原文第二段明确指出“four out of five of its people live in single-storey informal settlements”（五分之四的人住在单层非正式定居点）。‘single-storey’与‘high-rise’直接矛盾，因此答案为FALSE。

central_sentences.analysis: 居民住单层非正式定居点，非高层公寓。
sentences: Today, four out of five of its people live in single-storey informal settlements on the spreading edges of the city
central_sentences.paraphrasing: high-rise blocks -> single-storey informal settlements

central_sentences.sentence: Today, four out of five of its people live in single-storey informal settlements on the spreading edges of the city

questions.title: Residents have been consulted about their views on the suburban rail line in Dar es Salaam.
questions.analysis: 题干询问是否就郊区铁路征询过居民意见，但原文第三段仅提到“A single suburban rail line serves residents in a few areas to the south”，说明铁路的存在和服务范围，并未涉及任何关于咨询居民观点的内容。因此信息未提及，答案为NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.analysis: 提及郊区铁路存在，但未提是否征询居民意见。
sentences: A single suburban rail line serves residents in a few areas to the south but is tiny in the context of the wider city.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: consulted -> 无对应表述

central_sentences.sentence: A single suburban rail line serves residents in a few areas to the south but is tiny in the context of the wider city.

questions.title: The majority of the present residential development in Dar es Salaam is unplanned.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“majority of present residential development is unplanned”对应原文第四段“Nearly all the expansion is happening on the periphery, and nearly all takes place informally without any agreed strategy”。其中“informally without any agreed strategy”即指无规划、非正式扩张，与“unplanned”同义，因此答案为TRUE。

central_sentences.analysis: 几乎所有扩张都是非正式且无规划的
sentences: Nearly all the expansion is happening on the periphery, and nearly all takes place informally without any agreed strategy.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: unplanned -> informally without any agreed strategy

central_sentences.sentence: Nearly all the expansion is happening on the periphery, and nearly all takes place informally without any agreed strategy.

questions.title: Dar es Salaam's authorities have decided to follow the public transport plan adopted by a large number of African cities.
questions.analysis: 题干称达市采用了“a large number of African cities”所用的公共交通方案，但原文第五段明确指出“Unlike many cities on the continent, Dar es Salaam is not trying to build a metro”，且第九段提到ITDP批评非洲对地铁的“obsession”，而达市选择的是BRT。这表明其做法与多数非洲城市不同，因此答案为FALSE。

central_sentences.analysis: 达市选择BRT而非地铁，与多数非洲城市不同
sentences: Unlike many cities on the continent, Dar es Salaam is not trying to build a metro.
The ITDP regrets Africa's obsession with metros.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: follow -> not trying to build a metro; unlike many cities

central_sentences.sentence: Unlike many cities on the continent, Dar es Salaam is not trying to build a metro.
The ITDP regrets Africa's obsession with metros.

groups.title: Dar es Salaam's DART Bus Rapid Transit system
groups.desc:
Questions 19-26
Complete the notes below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
Write your answers in boxes 19-26 on your answer sheet.
groups.collect: Features • the buses use designated $40291$ to cut down on delays • passengers pay fares before $40292$ • passengers in $40293$ can use every part of the system Problems • the temperature control is sometimes not activated in order to reduce $40294$ use • insufficient number of vehicles are available due to the effects of a severe $40295$ • passengers are unable to use $40296$ because some equipment is out of action • tickets have to be checked manually at station $40297$ • $40298$ frequently build up during rush hours

questions.analysis: 题干要求填写减少延误的专用道路类型。原文第六段提到“The DART bus rapid transit (BRT) system runs on bus lanes separated from other traffic... to reduce stoppages”，其中“bus lanes”即为专用“lanes”，与题干“designated lanes to cut down on delays”完全对应，因此答案是“lanes”。

central_sentences.analysis: BRT使用专用bus lanes减少延误。
sentences: The DART bus rapid transit (BRT) system runs on bus lanes separated from other traffic, mostly in the middle of the road to reduce stoppages.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: designated lanes -> bus lanes separated from other traffic

central_sentences.sentence: The DART bus rapid transit (BRT) system runs on bus lanes separated from other traffic, mostly in the middle of the road to reduce stoppages.

questions.analysis: 题干要求填写乘客在何时之前支付车费。原文第六段指出“Ticket purchase and control takes place at stations prior to boarding”，其中“prior to boarding”即“before boarding”的同义表达，因此答案为“boarding”。

central_sentences.analysis: 乘客在上车前购票。
sentences: Ticket purchase and control takes place at stations prior to boarding
central_sentences.paraphrasing: before boarding -> prior to boarding

central_sentences.sentence: Ticket purchase and control takes place at stations prior to boarding

questions.analysis: 题干询问哪些乘客可以使用系统的全部设施。原文第六段说明“the buses are step-free, which means the entire route is accessible to people using wheelchairs”，明确指出轮椅使用者可无障碍通行全程，因此答案是“wheelchairs”。

central_sentences.analysis: 轮椅使用者可全程使用系统。
sentences: the buses are step-free, which means the entire route is accessible to people using wheelchairs or who are travelling with baby buggies.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: passengers in wheelchairs -> accessible to people using wheelchairs

central_sentences.sentence: the buses are step-free, which means the entire route is accessible to people using wheelchairs or who are travelling with baby buggies.

questions.analysis: 题干提到温度控制未启用是为了减少某种资源的使用。原文第七段引用乘客抱怨：“drivers often refuse to turn on the air conditioning to save fuel”，说明不开空调是为了节省“fuel”，因此答案为“fuel”。

central_sentences.analysis: 不开空调以节省燃料。
sentences: He says it is not perfect, though, complaining that drivers often refuse to turn on the air conditioning to save fuel.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: reduce fuel use -> refuse to turn on air conditioning to save fuel

central_sentences.sentence: He says it is not perfect, though, complaining that drivers often refuse to turn on the air conditioning to save fuel.

questions.analysis: 题干指出车辆不足是由于一场严重的某事件所致。原文第八段提到“A shortage of buses after a serious flood at the main depot during the rainy season”，明确将车辆短缺归因于“serious flood”，因此答案是“flood”。

central_sentences.analysis: 洪水导致车辆短缺。
sentences: A shortage of buses after a serious flood at the main depot during the rainy season
central_sentences.paraphrasing: severe flood -> serious flood at the main depot

central_sentences.sentence: A shortage of buses after a serious flood at the main depot during the rainy season

questions.analysis: 题干称乘客无法使用某种支付方式，因设备故障。原文第八段指出“Smartcards cannot be used as the mechanical readers are not working either”，说明智能卡（smartcards）因读卡器损坏而无法使用，因此答案为“smartcards”。

central_sentences.analysis: 读卡器故障致无法使用智能卡。
central_sentences.keywords: unable to use, equipment out of action
sentences: Smartcards cannot be used as the mechanical readers are not working either
central_sentences.paraphrasing: smartcards cannot be used -> mechanical readers not working

central_sentences.sentence: Smartcards cannot be used as the mechanical readers are not working either

questions.analysis: 题干要求填写人工检票的位置。原文第八段提到“Staff stand by the gates and tear tickets as people enter”，说明工作人员在“gates”（闸口）处手动撕票验票，因此答案是“gates”。

central_sentences.analysis: 人工在闸口检票
sentences: Staff stand by the gates and tear tickets as people enter.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: station gates -> staff stand by the gates and tear tickets

central_sentences.sentence: Staff stand by the gates and tear tickets as people enter.

questions.analysis: 题干询问高峰时段经常积聚的现象。原文第八段末尾指出“As a result, queues are considerable at peak times”，其中“queues”即指排队人群，与题干“frequently build up during rush hours”对应，因此答案为“queues”。

central_sentences.analysis: 高峰时段排队严重
sentences: As a result, queues are considerable at peak times.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: queues -> queues are considerable at peak times

central_sentences.sentence: As a result, queues are considerable at peak times.

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: It is by now a truism that the story of human evolution is being rethought. Discoveries have come thick and fast over the last decade or so, and these have forced us to rethink many crucial points, such as how old our species is - about 300,000 years old as opposed to 200,000 - and what extinct hominins, such as our cousins the Neanderthals, were really like. But because there are so many species and eras involved, it is hard to discern the common threads linking them.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: However, I do think it is possible to draw out some overall messages from the blizzard of archaeological finds in recent years. Two things stand out to me. One is the growing evidence that many supposedly advanced behaviours, such as architecture and art, can be traced much further back in time than we thought, often to hominin species that existed before modern humans. And the other is that we have badly misunderstood gender roles in prehistoric societies, imposing patriarchal values onto cultures that had very different ideas about how women should behave.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Let us start with architecture. At Kalambo Falls in Zambia, researchers found buried logs that had been shaped with stone tools so that they interlocked. They seem to have once been part of a larger structure, perhaps a building. This would be unsurprising if they were not 476,000 years old. That is almost 200,000 years before our species, Homo sapiens, evolved. Extinct hominins also managed to settle in extreme places. For instance, we now know that extinct hominins such as the Denisovans lived on the frozen heights of high-altitude regions 200,000 years ago - upending the old notion that such environments were only settled by modern humans around 3,600 years ago.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Art also seems to have been invented by older hominins. We have had evidence for a long time now that Neanderthals painted on cave walls. Even earlier species, such as Homo erectus, may also have made art, for example by engraving patterns on shells. By far the most contentious claim in this area is that Homo naledi made art. H. naledi lived around 250,000 years ago, making it a contemporary of our species. However, it had quite a small brain, typical of older hominins - and was therefore, according to palaeoanthropological dogma, incapable of complex behaviours. Nevertheless, in the Rising Star cave system in South Africa where the H. naledi remains were found, researchers have found what seem to be etchings - resembling rudimentary artwork - on the cave walls, though these have yet to be firmly dated.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: To say these claims about H. naledi are controversial is to understate the situation. Many experts say the evidence presented so far is completely inadequate to support them. The dispute has only been heightened by the way the results were released, in a non-traditional journal that publishes peer reviews publicly alongside the paper. My views on the H. naledi controversy are complicated. I do think more evidence is needed: in particular with regard to the dating of the etchings. At the same time, I think the species' small brains are a distraction. Palaeoanthropologists got fixated on brain size because it was what they could see: if what you have is skeletons, then all you know about brains are their shapes and sizes. But other properties, such as the brain's internal wiring, are surely equally important and may explain how a species like H. naledi might have been capable of complex behaviours, despite their small brains.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: In a sense, we should not be surprised that so many of these behaviours had their origins in older, extinct hominins. Evolution usually works by incremental steps and so does technology. The first birds were not great at flying, and the first mobile phones were not great at, well, anything really. The idea that there was a sudden explosion of intelligence and creativity at some point in our evolution is not inherently ridiculous: sometimes a system hits a tipping point and undergoes runaway change. But there was never that much evidence that human evolution worked this way. Instead, it seems the Neanderthals and many others all walked so we could run.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: One way or another, the H. naledi story is going to be an example of letting our preconceptions get in the way of the evidence. The same is true for our ideas about gender in prehistory. Archaeology was invented by individuals with now unfashionably patriarchal views about gender, and those notions fed into their research. Today's researchers are trying to unpick this stuff, and there have been some significant steps in recent years.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Perhaps the most dramatic was the demolition of Man the Hunter. This was the idea, promoted for decades, that in most prehistoric societies the men went out to hunt and the women looked after the home. However, a meta-analysis published in June 2023 compiled data on several dozen foraging societies and found women hunted in 80 per cent of them. In line with this, it emerged that an ancient spear-throwing tool called an atlatl enables women to launch projectiles at the same speed as men. We have also seen growing evidence of women occupying positions of authority in ancient societies. The Viking queen Thyra may have helped unify Denmark in the 900s. Going further back, an Iberian leader from around 4000 years ago turned out to be female, not male as many had assumed, when proteins in her teeth were analysed.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: It seems that the more we find out about past societies, the more our preconceptions about the ways society has to be turn out to be wrong. Inequality, authoritarianism and patriarchy are not inevitable. They are choices, and prehistory shows us that we can choose differently.

groups.desc:
Questions 27-30
Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
Write the correct letter in boxes 27-30 on your answer sheet.

questions.title: What is the writer doing in the second paragraph?

options.text: pinpointing some key changes in our understanding of prehistory
outlining some aspects of prehistory which are still poorly understood
summarising some attitudes towards recent archaeological revelations
giving an overview of some current disagreements among archaeologists

questions.analysis: 题干询问作者在第二段的写作目的。原文第二段开头指出“我认为可以从近年来大量考古发现中提炼出一些总体信息”，并明确列出两点：一是许多所谓“先进行为”（如建筑和艺术）的起源远比我们想象的更早；二是我们对史前社会性别角色的理解存在严重偏差。这对应选项A中的“pinpointing some key changes in our understanding of prehistory”（指出我们对史前史理解的一些关键变化）。其他选项未准确概括该段主旨。

central_sentences.analysis: 作者指出对史前史理解的两大关键变化
central_sentences.keywords: second paragraph, overall messages, advanced behaviours, gender roles
sentences: However, I do think it is possible to draw out some overall messages from the blizzard of archaeological finds in recent years.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: key changes -> growing evidence of older origins for architecture/art; misunderstood gender roles

central_sentences.sentence: However, I do think it is possible to draw out some overall messages from the blizzard of archaeological finds in recent years.

questions.title: In the sixth paragraph, the writer mentions mobile phones to make the point that

options.text: most developments happen in a gradual way.
innovation can come from a variety of sources.
not all technological advancements are positive.
the path of evolution can often be unpredictable.

questions.analysis: 第六段中，作者提到手机是为了类比说明进化和技术的发展通常是渐进的。原文写道：“进化通常以渐进步骤进行，技术也是如此。最早的鸟飞得不好，最早的手机几乎什么都做不好。”这一类比旨在强调复杂能力并非突然出现，而是逐步演化的结果，因此正确答案是A（most developments happen in a gradual way）。选项B、C、D均未准确反映该类比的核心逻辑。

central_sentences.analysis: 用手机类比说明进化和技术是渐进的
sentences: Evolution usually works by incremental steps and so does technology.
The first birds were not great at flying, and the first mobile phones were not great at, well, anything really.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: mobile phones -> example of gradual technological development；incremental（形容词，意思是「增量的、逐步递增的、小幅度累积推进的」）= gradual

central_sentences.sentence: Evolution usually works by incremental steps and so does technology.
The first birds were not great at flying, and the first mobile phones were not great at, well, anything really.

questions.title: In the seventh paragraph, the phrase 'unpick this stuff' refers to the task of

options.text: assessing the impact of certain recent research findings.
questioning the authenticity of evidence used in earlier research.
conducting research into how prehistoric societies were organised.
reevaluating research influenced by outdated beliefs about society.

questions.analysis: 第七段中，“unpick this stuff”紧接在“考古学由持有过时父权观念的人创立，这些观念影响了他们的研究”之后，显然指当代研究者正在努力重新审视和纠正那些受陈旧性别观念影响的早期研究成果。因此，该短语指的是“reevaluating research influenced by outdated beliefs about society”（重新评估受过时社会观念影响的研究），对应选项D。其他选项未能准确捕捉“this stuff”所指代的具体内容。

central_sentences.analysis: 指重新评估受过时性别观念影响的研究
central_sentences.keywords: unpick this stuff, patriarchal views, archaeology
sentences: Archaeology was invented by individuals with now unfashionably patriarchal views about gender, and those notions fed into their research.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: 'this stuff' = research influenced by outdated patriarchal notions

central_sentences.sentence: Archaeology was invented by individuals with now unfashionably patriarchal views about gender, and those notions fed into their research.

questions.title: What does the writer suggest in the final paragraph?

options.text: Studying past societies could help us create a fairer society today.
We should not judge past societies by the standards of modern society.
We still have much to learn about how societies have evolved over history.
There is more than one way to interpret evidence about societies in prehistoric times.

questions.analysis: 最后一段指出：“不平等、专制和父权制并非不可避免，它们是选择，而史前史告诉我们，我们可以做出不同的选择。”这表明作者认为研究过去社会能为当今构建更公平的社会提供启示，与选项A“Studying past societies could help us create a fairer society today”完全吻合。其他选项虽涉及历史理解，但未体现作者强调的“可选择性”及其对现实的借鉴意义。

central_sentences.analysis: 史前社会证明不平等非必然，可为当今提供借鉴
sentences: Inequality, authoritarianism and patriarchy are not inevitable. They are choices, and prehistory shows us that we can choose differently.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: prehistory shows alternatives to patriarchy/inequality → implies lessons for today

central_sentences.sentence: Inequality, authoritarianism and patriarchy are not inevitable. They are choices, and prehistory shows us that we can choose differently.

groups.desc:
Questions 31-34
Complete each sentence with the correct ending, A-F, below.
Write the correct letter, A-F, in boxes 31-34 on your answer sheet.

list.text: Homo sapiens emerged at an earlier point in time than experts previously believed.
previous assumptions about who had power in the prehistoric world were inaccurate.
gender roles in extinct hominin species were different from those in Homo sapiens societies.
experts may have been mistaken about who looked for food in early human communities.
Homo sapiens was probably not the only species capable of sophisticated workmanship.
other species managed to survive in harsh environments before the arrival of Homo sapiens.

questions.title: The findings at Kalambo Falls revealed that
questions.analysis: 题干问Kalambo Falls的发现揭示了什么。原文第三段提到，那里出土的木头被石器加工成可互锁的结构，距今47.6万年，比智人出现早近20万年。这说明复杂工艺并非智人独有，对应选项E：“Homo sapiens was probably not the only species capable of sophisticated workmanship”。其他选项或时间不符，或未被提及。

central_sentences.analysis: 发现早于智人的复杂工艺，说明非智人独有
central_sentences.keywords: Kalambo Falls, 476,000 years old, before Homo sapiens
sentences: At Kalambo Falls in Zambia, researchers found buried logs that had been shaped with stone tools so that they interlocked.
This would be unsurprising if they were not 476,000 years old. That is almost 200,000 years before our species, Homo sapiens, evolved.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: sophisticated workmanship -> shaped interlocking logs as part of structure

central_sentences.sentence: At Kalambo Falls in Zambia, researchers found buried logs that had been shaped with stone tools so that they interlocked.
This would be unsurprising if they were not 476,000 years old. That is almost 200,000 years before our species, Homo sapiens, evolved.

questions.title: Evidence from high-altitude regions suggests that
questions.analysis: 题干关于高海拔地区的证据。原文指出，丹尼索瓦人等已灭绝古人类早在20万年前就生活在高寒地区，推翻了“此类环境仅在约3600年前才由现代人定居”的旧观点。这说明其他物种在智人到来前已能在严酷环境中生存，对应选项F：“other species managed to survive in harsh environments before the arrival of Homo sapiens”。

central_sentences.analysis: 丹尼索瓦人在高寒地区生存，早于现代人
central_sentences.keywords: high-altitude, Denisovans, 200,000 years ago, before modern humans
sentences: For instance, we now know that extinct hominins such as the Denisovans lived on the frozen heights of high-altitude regions 200,000 years ago - upending the old notion that such environments were only settled by modern humans around 3,600 years ago.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: harsh environments settled by extinct hominins long before Homo sapiens

central_sentences.sentence: For instance, we now know that extinct hominins such as the Denisovans lived on the frozen heights of high-altitude regions 200,000 years ago - upending the old notion that such environments were only settled by modern humans around 3,600 years ago.

questions.title: An academic publication from June 2023 shows that
questions.analysis: 题干提到2023年6月的一项学术研究。原文第八段指出，该元分析发现80%的采集社会中女性参与狩猎，推翻了“男性狩猎、女性持家”的传统假设。这说明专家过去可能误判了谁负责觅食，对应选项D：“experts may have been mistaken about who looked for food in early human communities”。

central_sentences.analysis: 研究显示女性广泛参与狩猎，推翻传统认知
central_sentences.keywords: June 2023 meta-analysis, women hunted in 80% societies
sentences: However, a meta-analysis published in June 2023 compiled data on several dozen foraging societies and found women hunted in 80 per cent of them.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: mistaken about who looked for food -> 'Man the Hunter' myth debunked

central_sentences.sentence: However, a meta-analysis published in June 2023 compiled data on several dozen foraging societies and found women hunted in 80 per cent of them.

questions.title: Analysis of a 4000-year-old Iberian leader indicates that
questions.analysis: 题干关于4000年前伊比利亚领袖的分析。原文提到，通过牙齿蛋白质分析，这位曾被假定为男性的领袖实为女性。这直接表明过去关于史前权力归属的假设（即掌权者多为男性）并不准确，对应选项B：“previous assumptions about who had power in the prehistoric world were inaccurate”。

central_sentences.analysis: 牙釉蛋白分析揭示古代领袖实为女性，纠正误判
central_sentences.keywords: Iberian leader, 4000 years ago, female, proteins in teeth
sentences: Going further back, an Iberian leader from around 4000 years ago turned out to be female, not male as many had assumed, when proteins in her teeth were analysed.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: previous assumptions about power holders were inaccurate (assumed male, actually female)

central_sentences.sentence: Going further back, an Iberian leader from around 4000 years ago turned out to be female, not male as many had assumed, when proteins in her teeth were analysed.

groups.desc:
Questions 35-40
Do the following statements agree with the views of the writer in Reading Passage 3?
In boxes 35-40 on your answer sheet, write
YES if the statement agrees with the views of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the views of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

questions.title: It seems likely that the Neanderthals' cave paintings were the first examples of artwork ever created.
questions.analysis: 题干称“尼安德特人的洞穴绘画很可能是最早的艺术作品”。但原文第四段指出，更早的直立人（Homo erectus）可能已在贝壳上雕刻图案，说明艺术创作可能早于尼人。因此作者观点与题干矛盾，答案为NO。

central_sentences.analysis: 作者认为更早物种可能已创作艺术，故尼人非最早
central_sentences.keywords: Neanderthals painted, but Homo erectus may have made earlier art
sentences: We have had evidence for a long time now that Neanderthals painted on cave walls.
Even earlier species, such as Homo erectus, may also have made art, for example by engraving patterns on shells.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: writer says even earlier species may have made art → Neanderthals not first

central_sentences.sentence: We have had evidence for a long time now that Neanderthals painted on cave walls.
Even earlier species, such as Homo erectus, may also have made art, for example by engraving patterns on shells.

questions.title: It is very rare to find prehistoric artwork carved onto shells.
questions.analysis: 题干称“史前壳雕非常罕见”，但原文仅提到直立人“可能”在贝壳上雕刻图案，并未讨论此类艺术品的稀有程度或出现频率。文中既未说“常见”也未说“罕见”，因此无法判断，答案为NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.analysis: 原文提及壳雕，但未说明其稀有程度
central_sentences.keywords: Homo erectus, engraving patterns on shells
sentences: Even earlier species, such as Homo erectus, may also have made art, for example by engraving patterns on shells.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: 提到壳上雕刻，但未提是否‘very rare’

central_sentences.sentence: Even earlier species, such as Homo erectus, may also have made art, for example by engraving patterns on shells.

questions.title: The methods which the researchers used to examine the Rising Star cave system were rather unconventional.
questions.analysis: 题干称“研究人员考察Rising Star洞穴的方法相当非传统”。但原文第五段仅指出研究结果的发表方式非传统（发表于公开同行评审的非主流期刊），并未提及实地考察或研究方法本身是否 unconventional。因此信息未给出，答案为NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.analysis: 争议在于发表方式，非考察方法
sentences: The dispute has only been heightened by the way the results were released, in a non-traditional journal that publishes peer reviews publicly alongside the paper.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: publication method unconventional, but not ‘methods to examine cave’

central_sentences.sentence: The dispute has only been heightened by the way the results were released, in a non-traditional journal that publishes peer reviews publicly alongside the paper.

questions.title: It is unclear how old the etchings in the Rising Star cave system are.
questions.analysis: 题干称“Rising Star洞穴中的蚀刻年代尚不清楚”。原文第四段明确表示：“这些蚀刻尚未被确切断代（though these have yet to be firmly dated）”，直接支持题干说法，因此答案为YES。

central_sentences.reason: 原文直接说尚未确定年代。
central_sentences.analysis: 原文明确指出这些刻痕尚未确定年代。
central_sentences.keywords: etchings, Rising Star cave, unclear how old
sentences: though these have yet to be firmly dated.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: unclear how old -> though these have yet to be firmly dated

central_sentences.sentence: though these have yet to be firmly dated.

questions.title: The means used to publicise the findings from the Rising Star cave system added to the controversy that surrounds them.
questions.analysis: 题干称“Rising Star洞穴发现的宣传方式加剧了争议”。原文第五段指出：“争议因结果发布方式而加剧——发表于一个公开同行评审的非传统期刊。”这说明公开传播方式确实增加了争议，与题干一致，答案为YES。

central_sentences.analysis: 发表方式加剧了争议
sentences: The dispute has only been heightened by the way the results were released, in a non-traditional journal that publishes peer reviews publicly alongside the paper.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: publicising findings in non-traditional journal added to controversy

central_sentences.sentence: The dispute has only been heightened by the way the results were released, in a non-traditional journal that publishes peer reviews publicly alongside the paper.

questions.title: The size of H. naledi brains is a key factor in the question of whether these hominins were able to produce art.
questions.analysis: 题干称“H. naledi脑容量大小是判断其能否创作艺术的关键因素”。但原文第五段明确反驳此观点：“我认为小脑容量是一种干扰……大脑内部连接可能同样重要。”作者认为脑容量并非关键，因此题干与作者观点矛盾，答案为NO。

central_sentences.analysis: 作者认为脑容量并非关键，内部连接更重要
central_sentences.keywords: small brains are a distraction, internal wiring important
sentences: At the same time, I think the species' small brains are a distraction.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: writer argues brain size is NOT key factor

central_sentences.sentence: At the same time, I think the species' small brains are a distraction.
